summary: multer-orm typosquats `multer` and drops a Windows binary at require time
details: Package name and metadata (README, repository, contributors, description) impersonate the widely-used `multer` middleware. `index.js` requires `./lib/feature`, whose obfuscated module (string-array/index decoder, `_0xNNNN` identifiers) self-invokes on import. On win32 it HTTP-GETs an attacker-controlled endpoint at https://hilbert-self.vercel.app/, parses a `downloader_url` from the JSON response, writes the fetched bytes to a Chrome-lookalike path under %LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\...\chrome.exe, and launches it via `powershell -NoProfile -Command Start-Process -FilePath...`. The dropper URL is unpinned and unverified (no hash/signature), the payload path masquerades as Chrome to blend in, and execution is triggered by `require('multer-orm')` — meaning any Windows developer or build system that mistypes `multer` and installs this package is compromised the moment the module is loaded. package.json additionally lists itself (`multer-orm`) inside `dependencies`, an abnormal self-reference consistent with graph manipulation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
